hydross.SCORECARD_FINDING: This was the raid's most chaotic kill by far: 15 real deaths, including Fuggler's own, in a real near-wipe that still ended in a kill. HPS (615) ran well below the Top 100 average (1,477, a 42% ratio), landing at a middling 45th-percentile result - still enough to rank 1st of 5 tracked healers by item-level, though only 2nd by raw healing.
hydross.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The off-rank Chain Heal cast (guid 10623) ran 16.9 points ahead of its Top 100 benchmark share (18.6% vs 1.7%) - the start of a pattern that holds on every kill this raid night, with the benchmark's own top-rank Chain Heal (guid 25423) still leading Fuggler's own composition too (67.0%).
hydross.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Earth Shield, Mana Tide Totem, Ancestral Swiftness, and Mana Potion were all cast at least once, roughly in line with Top 100 norms. With 15 real deaths in a single short window, no individual cooldown timing reads as a specific missed save - this is raid-wide burst damage, not a targeted gap.
hydross.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (79.8%) tracked close to the Top 100 norm; top-target concentration (25.6% vs 47.5% average, -21.9) ran well below it, across 15 distinct targets - a wider spread than the benchmark's usual pattern on this fight.

lurker.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (785) ran below the Top 100 average (1,776, a 44% ratio), on a solid 54th-percentile kill, ranking 2nd of 5 tracked healers by both item-level rank and raw healing, on the raid's second-most chaotic kill (12 real deaths).
lurker.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The off-rank Chain Heal cast (guid 10623) ran 28.5 points ahead of its Top 100 benchmark share (31.4% vs 2.9%) - continuing the same pattern seen on Hydross, with the top-rank Chain Heal (guid 25423) still leading overall (59.8%).
lurker.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Every tracked cooldown here matched Top 100 norms - Earth Shield, Mana Tide Totem, Ancestral Swiftness, and Mana Potion all landed in line. With 12 real deaths, none lines up closely enough with a specific cooldown to call a real save.
lurker.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (53.5%) and top-target concentration (14.0%) both tracked close to the Top 100 norm, across the raid's widest spread (23 distinct targets).

leotheras.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (875) ran below the Top 100 average (1,990, a 44% ratio), on a solid 58th-percentile kill, ranking 1st of 5 tracked healers by item-level rank and 2nd by raw healing, with 10 real deaths logged on this real chaotic encounter.
leotheras.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The off-rank Chain Heal cast (guid 10623) ran 38.9 points ahead of its Top 100 benchmark share (40.2% vs 1.3%) - the largest such gap so far this raid, with the top-rank Chain Heal (guid 25423) still leading overall (53.9%).
leotheras.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Earth Shield, Mana Tide Totem, Ancestral Swiftness (2 casts), and Mana Potion were all cast, roughly in line with or slightly above Top 100 norms. With 10 real deaths, none lines up closely enough with a specific cooldown to call a real save - this reads as raid-wide damage, not a missed opportunity.
leotheras.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (66.0% vs 51.2% average, +14.8) and top-target concentration (19.5% vs 13.9% average, +5.6) both ran above the Top 100 norm across 19 distinct targets - a more concentrated healing pattern than the benchmark typically shows on this fight.

karathress.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (997) ran below the Top 100 average (2,177, a 46% ratio), on a middling 51st-percentile kill, ranking 2nd of 5 tracked healers by item-level rank and 3rd by raw healing, with only 1 real death logged (Megatauro) - the cleanest kill of the raid's first half.
karathress.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The off-rank Chain Heal cast (guid 10623) ran 19.6 points ahead of its Top 100 benchmark share; a third real rank (guid 1064) also carried a real 12.0% of this kill's healing, not present in the top rows of most other kills this raid.
karathress.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Earth Shield and Ancestral Swiftness both went uncast against real 67%/86% Top 100 usage norms - the raid's clearest cooldown gap. Megatauro's death has no cooldown nearby worth calling a save - the nearest cast (a self-targeted Mana Potion) landed 3.7 seconds later.
karathress.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (53.8% vs 71.7% average, -17.9) and top-target concentration (15.6% vs 35.3% average, -19.7) both ran well below the Top 100 norm across 23 distinct targets - the benchmark sample concentrates more heavily on a single target on this fight than Fuggler did here.

morogrim.SCORECARD_FINDING: The raid's low point: HPS (652) ran well below the Top 100 average (1,546, a 42% ratio), and overheal (49.5%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse - one of only two kills this raid where that happens - landing at a weak 26th-percentile result, the raid's lowest, ranking 4th of 5 tracked healers by both item-level rank and raw healing. 3 real deaths logged.
morogrim.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The off-rank Chain Heal cast (guid 10623) ran 34.4 points ahead of its Top 100 benchmark share (37.5% vs 3.1%), continuing the same pattern seen all raid night.
morogrim.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Every tracked cooldown here matched Top 100 norms in count. None of the 3 real deaths lines up closely with a cooldown that could plausibly have prevented it - the nearest casts (Earth Shield, aimed at a different target each time) are coincidental timing.
morogrim.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (54.6%) and top-target concentration (22.6%) both tracked in line with the Top 100 norm, across the raid's widest spread (25 distinct targets).

vashj.SCORECARD_FINDING: The raid's standout kill: HPS (761) reached 70% of the Top 100 average (1,091) - the closest ratio to benchmark of the raid - producing a strong 84th-percentile result, the raid's best. Fuggler ranked 1st of 5 tracked healers by both item-level rank and raw healing here, the only kill this raid with that combination. 6 real deaths logged on the raid's longest fight (498 seconds).
vashj.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The off-rank Chain Heal cast (guid 10623) carried 65.5% of this kill's healing, 61.2 points ahead of its Top 100 benchmark share (4.3%) - the largest single-spell gap of the entire raid, with the usual top-rank Chain Heal (guid 25423) running a comparatively modest 16.7% here, well under its own 48.5% Top 100 share.
vashj.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Earth Shield (4 casts, all on Braila), Mana Tide Totem, Ancestral Swiftness, and Mana Potion were all cast; Dark Rune went uncast against a real 51% Top 100 usage rate - the one real cooldown gap this kill. None of the 6 real deaths lines up closely with a cooldown that could plausibly have prevented it - the nearest casts (Earth Shield, aimed at Braila specifically) are coincidental timing.
vashj.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (43.5%) and top-target concentration (12.5%) both tracked close to the Top 100 norm, across the raid's second-widest spread (26 distinct targets).

alar.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (528) ran well below the Top 100 average (1,132, a 47% ratio), but this was still a strong 64th-percentile kill, ranking 1st of 5 tracked healers by item-level rank and 2nd by raw healing, with 4 real deaths logged.
alar.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The off-rank Chain Heal cast (guid 10623) carried 50.7% of this kill's healing, 47.3 points ahead of its Top 100 benchmark share (3.4%) - the second-largest such gap of the raid.
alar.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Earth Shield (4 casts, all on Braila), Mana Tide Totem, Ancestral Swiftness, and Mana Potion were all cast, roughly in line with Top 100 norms. None of the 4 real deaths lines up closely with a cooldown that could plausibly have prevented it.
alar.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (71.9% vs 54.7% average, +17.2) and top-target concentration (35.9% vs 23.8% average, +12.1) both ran above the Top 100 norm, concentrated heavily on Braila (35.9% of total healing, the raid's highest single-target concentration) across 23 distinct targets.

voidreaver.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (1,531, the raid's highest raw HPS) still ran below the Top 100 average (2,664, a 57% ratio), and overheal (18.3%) was the raid's lowest by a wide margin - on a decent 52nd-percentile kill, ranking 3rd of 5 tracked healers by both item-level rank and raw healing, with 2 real deaths logged.
voidreaver.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The off-rank Chain Heal cast (guid 10623) ran 28.1 points ahead of its Top 100 benchmark share (29.0% vs 0.9%), continuing the same pattern seen all raid night.
voidreaver.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Every tracked cooldown here matched Top 100 norms - Earth Shield, Mana Tide Totem, Ancestral Swiftness, and Mana Potion all landed in line. Neither real death (Silkyrebel, Leechers) lines up closely with a cooldown that could plausibly have prevented it.
voidreaver.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (44.5%) and top-target concentration (12.0%) both tracked in line with the Top 100 norm, across 22 distinct targets.

solarian.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (610) ran well below the Top 100 average (1,670, a 37% ratio), and overheal (61.8%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse - the second of two kills this raid where that happens - landing at a weak 40th-percentile kill, the raid's second-lowest, ranking 4th of 5 tracked healers by item-level rank and 3rd by raw healing. Zero real deaths logged, a clean kill despite the low percentile.
solarian.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The off-rank Chain Heal cast (guid 10623) ran 27.9 points ahead of its Top 100 benchmark share (30.1% vs 2.2%), the same pattern seen on every kill this raid night.
solarian.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Ancestral Swiftness went uncast against a real 71% Top 100 usage norm - the only real cooldown gap this kill. No deaths to answer.
solarian.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (45.6%) and top-target concentration (12.6%) both tracked in line with the Top 100 norm, across 22 distinct targets.

RaidOverview.GEAR_CONSISTENCY_FINDING: Fully consistent across all 9 kills - no slot varies at all, and all 9 enchantable slots carry a permanent enchant on every kill - the cleanest gear result seen across any of the reports generated so far.
RaidOverview.GEAR_FINDING_NOTE: No missing-enchant gaps this raid night. Consumables tell a different story: Guardian Elixir was never active on any of the 9 kills, and neither was Flask - only Battle Elixir (Healing Power) and Well Fed food were the consistent pairing, the same incomplete pattern already seen in other reports for this raid tier.
RaidOverview.RAID_SUMMARY_FINDING: 9 of 10 real bosses attempted this raid night appear in Fuggler's own data (the report shows 10 real bosses attempted overall; Fuggler simply wasn't present for the 10th). This was a genuinely strong raid night by rank: Fuggler ranked 1st of 5 tracked healers present by item-level rank on 4 of 9 kills (Hydross, Leotheras, Vashj, Al'ar; 53% average rank percentile) and 1st by raw healing on 1 of 9 (Vashj, the raid's standout kill at the 84th percentile - the only kill combining both 1st-place ranks). The single most consistent pattern all raid night: an off-rank Chain Heal cast (guid 10623) ran well ahead of its own Top 100 benchmark share on every one of the 9 kills, from +16.9 points (Hydross) to +61.2 points (Vashj, the largest composition gap of the raid). Percentiles ranged from 26th (Morogrim, the raid's lowest) to 84th (Vashj), with Morogrim, Solarian (40th), and Hydross (45th) the three lowest kills; overheal exceeded the Top 100 sample's own worst parse on 2 of 9 kills (Morogrim, Solarian). Fuggler died once this raid, on Hydross - the raid's most chaotic kill by far, with 15 real deaths in total. Hydross (15), Lurker (12), and Leotheras (10) were unusually death-heavy kills; Solarian was the only completely clean kill.
RaidOverview.RAID_WARNING_BANNER: Hydross saw 15 real deaths this raid, including Fuggler's own - the raid's most chaotic kill by a wide margin, though it still ended in a real kill. Guardian Elixir was never active on any of the 9 kills, matching the same consumable gap seen in other reports for this raid tier.

GearCheckItems.Description: All 9 enchantable slots carry a permanent enchant on every kill
GearCheckItems.LongDetail: No missing-enchant gaps this raid night - a real, clean result confirmed by the mechanical gear analysis.

GearCheckItems.Description: No Guardian Elixir active on any kill; no Flask either
GearCheckItems.LongDetail: Only Battle Elixir (Healing Power) and Well Fed food were consistently active across all 9 kills this raid night. Guardian Elixir was never up - the same incomplete consumable pattern already confirmed in other reports for this raid tier.
